statement: There is currently no accepted single correlate of malaria vaccine-induced protection.

statement: Low-transmission settings may require prohibitively large sample sizes to measure efficacy against clinical endpoints.

source_locator.quote: In low-transmission settings, low incidence rates are likely to result in prohibitively large sample sizes to achieve adequate statistical power to determine efficacy against clinical end-points.

statement: Post-licensure Phase 4 studies can provide additional data on routine-use safety, generalizability of efficacy, and confirmation of transmission-reducing effects.

source_locator.quote: post-licensure Phase 4 studies can provide critical additional data. statement: Any surrogate endpoint for transmission reduction in humans requires analytical and biological validation and regulatory approval.

source_locator.quote: Any candidate measure will require sufficient analytical and biological validation, as well as approval by regulatory authorities, if such data are to be used as surrogate end-points for transmission reduction in humans.

statement: Malaria vaccine studies should be conducted across the transmission intensities and seasonal variation settings in which the vaccine is intended for use.

statement: Acceptable malaria vaccine safety levels vary by indication and must be balanced against expected cases averted or disease incidence prevented in the setting.

statement: Variations in production process and vaccine components can affect efficacy, safety, dosing, cost, and stability.

source_locator.quote: Variations in production process and vaccine components can affect many aspects of the product profile, including efficacy, safety, dosing, cost and stability.

statement: It is important to establish a well-characterized production process in Phase 1.

source_locator.quote: It is important to establish a well-characterized production process in Phase 1

statement: Commercial-scale manufacturing capability generally starts early in Phase 2, requiring resolution of scale-up, formulation, release specifications, and presentation issues.

source_locator.quote: Generally, building commercial-scale manufacturing capability is started early in Phase 2 (147) . Therefore, issues related to scale-up, final formulation, release specifications and product presentation must be resolved.

statement: Ideally, Phase 3 studies use product from the final production facilities.

source_locator.quote: Ideally, Phase 3 studies are conducted using product from the final production facilities.

statement: New malaria vaccine introduction should align with existing delivery mechanisms and account for trade-offs with other vaccine distribution or malaria control programmes.

source_locator.quote: Alignment with existing delivery mechanisms and potential trade-offs with other vaccine distribution or malaria control intervention programmes need to be considered

statement: The lack of a dual high-income and LMIC market for malaria vaccines makes Phase 3 efficacy trials and commercial production financially unsustainable for industry.

source_locator.quote: the lack of a dual market (targeting both high-income countries and LMICs) for malaria vaccines makes investment in Phase 3 efficacy trials and commercial production and manufacturing financially unsustainable for industry

statement: Because malaria vaccines will be used almost exclusively in LMIC markets, programmatic suitability and sustainable access in endemic countries are critical.

source_locator.quote: any malaria vaccine will be E C V A E almost exclusively used in low- and middle-income markets, where the assessment of V D A L I R A programmatic suitability and sustainable access in endemic countries will be critical.

statement: RTS,S Phase 3 trials showed that a moderate-efficacy malaria vaccine can have considerable impact.

source_locator.quote: RTS,S Phase 3 trials have shown that, even with moderate vaccine efficacy, RTS,S has the potential for considerable impact.

statement: Over four years in Phase 3 trials, RTS,S averted more than 4000 clinical malaria cases per 1000 four-dose vaccinees in high-transmission settings such as Nanaro, Burkina Faso and Siaya, Kenya.

source_locator.quote: RTS,S was able to avert more than 4000 clinical malaria cases per 1000 vaccinees (receiving four doses) in high-transmission settings such as Nanaro, Burkina Faso and Siaya, Kenya.

statement: RTS,S had an estimated incremental cost-effectiveness ratio of US$25 per clinical case averted, with a range of US$16–222.

source_locator.quote: With an estimated incremental cost-effectiveness ratio (ICER) of US$ 25 (range US$ 16–222) per clinical case averted

statement: Malaria vaccines can achieve rapid scale-up and higher coverage through established routine EPI services compared with other malaria prevention tools.

source_locator.quote: malaria vaccines can achieve rapid scale-up and higher coverage through established and functioning routine EPI services compared to other malaria prevention tools.
